identity.short_name: Triple products of squared roots via reciprocal Newton sums (quartic)

problem_spec.summary: Given a monic quartic polynomial with roots r1,...,r4, compute the symmetric sum of triple products of squared roots: \sum_{i<j<k} r_i^2 r_j^2 r_k^2. Reduce it to a power sum of reciprocal roots using complementary products and then compute that power sum via Newton identities on the reciprocal polynomial.
givens: P(x) = x^4 - 2x^3 + x^2 + 3x + 2 is monic of degree 4.
r1,r2,r3,r4 are the complex roots of P(x), counted with multiplicity.
ask: Compute T = \sum_{1 \leq i < j < k \leq 4} r_i^2 r_j^2 r_k^2 as an integer.

parameter(s).name: degree
parameter(s).purpose: fixes Newton-identity depth and the symmetric-expression arity (triples of 4 roots).

parameter(s).name: coefficients
parameter(s).domain: (a1,a2,a3,a4) \in ℤ^4 for a monic quartic P(x)=x^4+a1 x^3+a2 x^2+a3 x+a4, with a4\neq0
parameter(s).purpose: controls the root symmetric sums; nonzero constant term enables reciprocal-root methods.
